growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

sec.method: chemostat, cell-specific CH4 production Vmax pooled across dilution rates/temperatures; headspace+dissolved CH4 by GC-FID

yields.note: no biomass yield reported; cell-specific rates only

growth_records.cite: Lucy C. Stewart et al. Fluid geochemistry, local hydrology, and metabolic activity define methanogen community size and composition in deep-sea hydrothermal vents. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0382-3. [PMC6776001]
growth_records.snippet: For M. jannaschii, the maximum CH4 production rate (Vmax) was 43 +/- 5 fmol CH4 cell-1 h-1 (+/- standard error) and the KS for H2 was 37 +/- 15 uM. ... the activation energy (Ea) was 86.3 kJ mol-1 and the pre-exponential factor (A) was 9.16
growth_records.notes: Experimental chemostat Monod: Vmax(CH4)=43+/-5 fmol/cell/h, KS(H2)=37+/-15 uM (rates temperature-indistinguishable so pooled across 82 and 65 C runs). Uptake derived from methanogenesis stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*43=172 fmol/cell/h, qCO2=1*43=43 fmol/cell/h (derived, is_experimental=false). Growth-rate is CALCULATED from the paper's batch (Balch-tube) Arrhenius fit mu=A*exp(-Ea/RT) with A=9.16e12 /h, Ea=86300 J/mol, R=8.314, T=355.15 K (82 C): Ea/RT=86300/(8.314*355.15)=29.23; mu=9.16e12*exp(-29.23)=9.16e12*2.02e-13=1.85 /h; td=ln2/1.85=0.375 h. Batch growth measured over 84-45 C. Actual chemostat dilution rates are in Table S2 (not in main text).
